vectors.name: Default Implicit Typing (SINGLE)
vectors.description: By default, QBasic treats all undeclared variables without a type suffix as Single-precision floats (!). They retain fractional values without rounding.

quirks_and_tests.description: Ensures the engine natively defaults to Single precision when no DEF type or suffix is active.

vectors.name: DEFINT Statement
vectors.description: Sets the default data type for variables to Integer.

quirks_and_tests.description: Forces all untyped variables starting with specified letters to automatically become integers. Floating-point assignments to these variables are mathematically rounded (Banker's rounding).

vectors.name: DEFSNG Statement
vectors.description: Sets the default data type for variables to Single-precision float.

quirks_and_tests.description: Forces untyped variables to default to Single-precision. This preserves fractional values without mathematical rounding. While QBasic defaults to Single precision natively, DEFSNG is used to explicitly override a previous DEFINT.
Even if DEFINT is active, appending a specific type suffix (like ! for Single Precision Float) overrides the implicit rule and preserves the fractional value.

vectors.name: Memory Aliasing (DIM AS STRING)
vectors.description: Declares a variable and allocates memory space.

quirks_and_tests.description: Declaring a variable with an explicit type (e.g., AS STRING) internally links it to its MS-DOS suffixed counterpart. Writing to the base name updates the suffixed name, as they share the exact same memory pointer.

vectors.name: Fixed-Length String Padding
vectors.description: Declares a string with a fixed memory length.

quirks_and_tests.description: Allocating a Fixed-Length String locks a specific memory block. If a shorter string is assigned, it is automatically padded with spaces on the right to fill the block.
If a string longer than the locked memory block is assigned, MS-DOS silently truncates the overflow without throwing an error.

vectors.name: SWAP Statement
vectors.description: Exchanges the values of two variables.

quirks_and_tests.description: Both variables must be of the same data type. Sysclone enforces deep cloning to ensure JavaScript object references don't bleed during the swap.

vectors.name: ERASE Statement (Static Arrays)
vectors.description: Reinitializes elements of static arrays or deallocates dynamic arrays.

quirks_and_tests.description: For static (non-dynamic) arrays, it does not destroy the array structure but soft-resets its elements: numbers revert to 0 and strings to empty.
